news.title: Wallabies vs Ireland live: Captain’s try scrubbed out in contentious obstruction call

sections.heading: Seruvakula demands accountability after England blowout
sections.body: Head coach Senirusi Seruvakula has issued a blunt warning to his squad following their heavy Round two defeat. With just one match remaining in their Nations Championship campaign, the coach has challenged his players to evaluate their own commitment. "Then we have to look at ourselves and if we really want to play and wear that jersey next week against Scotland," Seruvakula warned.

sections.heading: No fresh injury concerns reported in camp
sections.body: There is no fresh injury or squad news in today's coverage as the Flying Fijians prepare to face Scotland. With the squad coming off a bruising encounter, physical preparation remains confidential ahead of the selection announcement. The team continues their UK-based residency as they seek to bounce back from their heavy loss to England.

sections.heading: Scotland look to rebound after Springboks defeat
sections.body: Scotland return to Murrayfield after suffering a 42-28 loss to South Africa in their previous outing. Head coach Gregor Townsend has urged his squad to keep believing despite the defeat. However, the hosts face selection issues, with an unnamed Glasgow lock ruled out for weeks ahead of the match, while their vice-captain must undergo assessments after a failed head injury assessment.

sections.heading: Flying Fijians seek first competition points
sections.body: Fiji go into the weekend propping up the bottom of the Nations Championship log in 12th place after consecutive defeats. With zero wins from two matches, the Flying Fijians desperately need a positive result at Murrayfield to avoid finishing the inaugural tournament in last place. A win would provide a crucial financial and sporting boost.

sections.heading: Quirke called up as pressure mounts for Pollock start
sections.body: Sale Sharks scrum-half Raffi Quirke has joined the squad following the injury to Alex Mitchell. Meanwhile, head coach Steve Borthwick faces growing calls to name Henry Pollock in his starting lineup for the clash in Santiago del Estero. Pollock made a massive impact off the bench against Fiji, scoring a second-half hat-trick in the commanding 73-8 victory in Liverpool.

sections.heading: Mitchell ruled out of Argentina Test with hamstring injury
sections.body: Scrum-half Alex Mitchell has been ruled out of Saturday's Test against Argentina after sustaining a hamstring injury. Mitchell was forced off during the 73-8 win over Fiji in Liverpool, ending his participation in the remainder of the Nations Championship. No other injuries or recovery updates were reported in today's coverage, leaving Borthwick to finalize his options around his newly recalled halfbacks.

sections.heading: Los Pumas hunt home upset in Santiago del Estero
sections.body: Argentina represent a far stiffer challenge for England than Fiji did. Los Pumas bounced back from an opening-round defeat against Scotland to beat Wales 35-21 last weekend. They will host England at the Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, aiming to snap a five-match losing streak against Borthwick's side, which includes two narrow defeats during the 2025 matches.

sections.heading: England chase top-half climb in final first-leg fixture
sections.body: England enter the weekend seventh in the Nations Championship table with one win and one defeat. A victory in Santiago del Estero would push them into the upper half of the standings before the tournament pauses until November. Conversely, a defeat against Los Pumas risks sliding further down the log, undoing all the momentum built from the 12-try romp in Liverpool.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Grandidier Nkanang shines as France bounce back in style
sections.body: French spirits are high following a dominant 42-26 victory over Australia. Les Bleus found their clinical edge, running in six tries including a sensational brace from debutant winger Aaron Grandidier Nkanang. Emmanuel Meafou, Romain Ntamack, Florian Verhaeghe, and Théo Attissogbe also crossed the line, signaling that France's attacking depth remains formidable even during this demanding Southern Hemisphere tour.

sections.heading: Penaud officially ruled out of Saturday's Test against Japan
sections.body: Winger Damian Penaud remains sidelined and is officially ruled out of Saturday's Test against Japan. The prolific finisher suffered a calf injury during France's tournament-opening 34-32 loss, forcing him to watch the remainder of the Nations Championship from the sidelines. In his absence, the coaching staff continues to focus on integrating the squad's emerging outside backs into their high-tempo tactical drills.

sections.heading: Brave Blossoms target upset after falling to Ireland in Newcastle
sections.body: Japan enters the fixture looking to rebound from a 36-20 defeat against Ireland in Newcastle. Despite the loss, the Brave Blossoms showed attacking spark with tries from Taira and Hayate Era, alongside 10 points from the boot of Matsunaga. Japan historically plays without fear and will look to disrupt France's defensive line with their signature high-tempo, expansive style.

sections.heading: Les Bleus sit fourth as they chase consecutive victories
sections.body: France goes into the weekend fourth in the standings after bouncing back with a crucial victory in round two. Having split their opening matches with one win and one defeat, Les Bleus must secure another positive result against Japan to keep their finals aspirations alive. A second consecutive win would solidify their position in the upper tier of the 12-team table.

sections.heading: Sean Jansen in frame to face birth nation
sections.body: Breakout back-rower Sean Jansen has rocketed into contention for Saturday's clash against New Zealand. After an eye-catching debut try in the 36-20 win over Japan in Newcastle, the 27-year-old is knocking on the door to face his home country at Eden Park. Jansen, whose mother is Irish, only left New Zealand rugby four years ago before rising rapidly through the ranks.

sections.heading: Baloucoune on track after hamstring rest
sections.body: Robert Baloucoune is expected to be available for Saturday's Test after managing a hamstring issue. Head coach Andy Farrell has little doubt the winger will be fit to return after resting his tight hamstring during the victory over Japan. The squad's training has prioritized careful load management as the players build intensity for the physical challenge ahead in Auckland.

sections.heading: All Blacks protect formidable Eden Park record
sections.body: New Zealand present a daunting challenge at their Auckland fortress, where they remain undefeated in more than 50 matches since 1994. The hosts enter the fixture in clinical form after thrashing Italy 47-17, a match featuring a record-breaking hat-trick by Will Jordan. Head coach Dave Rennie is taking nothing for granted, noting his side is "well aware of Ireland's ability."

sections.heading: Ireland hunt historic Auckland triumph
sections.body: Ireland go into the weekend third in the Nations Championship table after securing two bonus-point wins from their opening matches. Confronting the All Blacks on their home turf represents the ultimate test for Andy Farrell's squad. A landmark victory in Auckland would not only shatter New Zealand's legendary stadium record but also keep Ireland firmly in the title hunt.

sections.heading: Niccolò Cannone banned for four matches
sections.body: Lock Niccolò Cannone has been hit with a four-match suspension following his red card against New Zealand last weekend. The disciplinary ruling rules him out of the remainder of the Nations Championship, starting with this weekend's clash against Australia. The coaching staff must now reshape the tight five as the Azzurri seek to rebound from their heavy second-round defeat.

sections.heading: Recovery focus as squad transitions to Perth
sections.body: With no fresh injury news in today's coverage, the squad has prioritized active recovery and physical conditioning after a bruising encounter against the All Blacks. The medical staff is concentrating on managing player fatigue following the long journey to Australia. The focus remains on getting the starting pack fully prepared for the intense set-piece battle awaiting them on Saturday.

sections.heading: Australia gamble on rookie playmaker
sections.body: Australia coach Joe Schmidt has resisted calling up veteran playmakers, opting for another rookie at fly-half despite their recent loss to France. The Wallabies are desperate for control after their inexperienced halves faltered last weekend, though they could be boosted if a key playmaker wins his fitness race. The hosts view the upcoming Test as a must-win fixture.

sections.heading: Azzurri seek to climb off the bottom
sections.body: Italy goes into the weekend sitting 11th in the tournament standings after consecutive defeats. Having conceded 74 points across their opening matches, the Azzurri desperately need a victory to kickstart their campaign. Defeating Australia on the road would not only lift Italy off the bottom of the log but also replicate their historic 26-19 victory over the Wallabies in Udine.

sections.heading: Townsend eyes response as camp returns to Edinburgh
sections.body: Gregor Townsend’s side are back at Murrayfield this week to begin preparations for the upcoming fixture against Fiji. Following the 42-28 loss to South Africa, the squad has been urged to "keep believing" as they shift their focus toward the next round. Management is currently assessing the group after a testing physical encounter in South Africa as they look to rotate back to winning form.

sections.heading: Assessment ongoing following Springboks test
sections.body: No fresh injury news was reported in today’s coverage regarding the Scotland squad following the Round 2 defeat against South Africa. With the group having only recently returned to their training base, staff are monitoring player wellbeing ahead of the team announcement later this week. Updates regarding individual availability will be clarified once the medical team completes their initial post-match screenings.

sections.heading: Fiji bring unique home atmosphere to Murrayfield
sections.body: In a scheduling quirk for the inaugural Nations Championship, Fiji will play this fixture as a designated home game despite the location. The team has been basing themselves in the UK for the opening half of the tournament to manage travel logistics and maximize commercial revenue. They are aiming to create a vibrant, festival-like atmosphere in Edinburgh to connect with their Northern Hemisphere diaspora supporters.

sections.heading: Scotland target climb in competitive mid-table
sections.body: Sitting 6 of 12 in the log, Scotland currently hold one win from two outings with a points difference of -5. A victory this weekend is essential for Townsend’s side to stabilize their campaign and climb the table. Having conceded 80 points across the first two rounds, defensive discipline will be a primary requirement to secure the points needed to move toward the top half.

sections.heading: Squad rotation and injury concerns
sections.body: Wales are managing a significant injury blow to captain Dewi Lake, who is set to miss the upcoming Test against South Africa. Coaching staff have called up a replacement hooker to join the squad in South Africa following the injury. Head coach Steve Tandy is expected to announce the starting lineup for the weekend fixture on Thursday.

sections.heading: Hooker concerns ahead of Durban
sections.body: Dewi Lake is the primary concern for the coaching staff after picking up an injury that puts him out of contention for Saturday. The team has responded by flying in an additional player to bolster the position. No other specific injury updates regarding the broader squad have been provided in today's media reports from the tour.

sections.heading: Springboks name four debutants
sections.body: South Africa have confirmed their team for Saturday, including four debutants in the starting XV: wing Jaco Williams, fly-half Vusi Moyo, lock Ruben van Heerden, and prop Carlu Sadie. Rassie Erasmus has made 10 changes to his side, with Pieter-Steph du Toit named as captain. The hosts are currently undefeated in the competition following wins over England and Scotland.

sections.heading: Rebounding in the Nations Championship
sections.body: Wales enter the third round of the Nations Championship sitting 8th on the log with one win and one loss. Following a 35-21 defeat to Argentina, the side looks to improve on their current points difference of plus 1. A result against the Springboks is essential to gain momentum as the tournament reaches its midway point.

sections.heading: Preparations intensify after Wales victory
sections.body: Following last weekend’s 35-21 success against Wales, the squad has returned to the training pitch to build momentum for Saturday’s Test. The coaching staff is focusing on tactical refinements as they prepare to host England in Santiago del Estero. No formal squad announcements have been made on Tuesday, as the group continues to integrate lessons from their recent win to counter the opposition’s physical game.

sections.heading: Conditioning remains the primary focus
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the squad. The team is undergoing standard recovery and non-contact conditioning sessions as they move into the mid-week phase of their preparation. Medical staff are monitoring all players closely, with training intensity scheduled to ramp up as the side aims to maintain the health of the core group ahead of the weekend.

sections.heading: Quirke called up as England make changes
sections.body: England have confirmed the arrival of scrum-half Raffi Quirke, who replaces Alex Mitchell after the latter sustained a hamstring injury during their recent victory over Fiji. Steve Borthwick’s side arrives with confidence following a high-scoring win, though they face pressure to rotate their pack. England have triumphed in their last 5 meetings with Los Pumas, looking to extend that streak this weekend.

sections.heading: Aiming to climb the tournament log
sections.body: Argentina currently sit 5th in the Nations Championship table with 1 win from their opening 2 matches. With 73 points scored and 68 conceded, the team holds a positive points difference of +5. A victory this weekend is essential for Los Pumas to improve their standing before the tournament breaks until November, as they aim to capitalize on home advantage against the touring English side.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Schmidt stands by youth for Italy clash
sections.body: Coach Joe Schmidt has resisted calls to recall veteran playmakers, instead opting to blood another rookie for the upcoming Test against Italy. Despite the influence of inexperienced halves in the recent loss to France, Schmidt remains committed to his current squad. Attention now turns to whether a key playmaker currently in the camp can prove their fitness in time for Saturday.

sections.heading: Playmaker in race for selection
sections.body: The Wallabies camp is monitoring the fitness of a key playmaker who remains in a race against time to be available for the weekend. There is no further fresh injury news in today's coverage as the squad focuses on recovery following their Round 2 fixture. Medical staff are expected to provide an update on the player's availability as training intensity ramps up.

sections.heading: Italy proving a dangerous proposition
sections.body: Italy arrives in Perth with momentum after a competitive showing against the All Blacks last weekend. The visitors have historically troubled the Wallabies, having secured victories over Australia in recent meetings, including success driven by players with Australian heritage. Given their recent form, the Italians represent a significant threat to a side that has struggled for consistency throughout the 2026 campaign.

sections.heading: Must-win encounter for bottom-tier Wallabies
sections.body: Australia sits 10th on the tournament log with two losses from two matches. With a points difference of -18, Saturday’s Test against Italy is effectively a must-win to revitalize their Nations Championship campaign. Following a difficult run where the team has lost nine of their past 10 Tests, securing a result in Perth is essential to shift the current trajectory of the season.

sections.heading: Selection process follows Ireland defeat
sections.body: Japan continues preparation following the 20-36 loss against Ireland last Saturday. With the next fixture against France approaching, the coaching staff is currently evaluating the squad to determine the lineup. No announcements have been made regarding team selection for the upcoming round of the Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Status of squad remains unconfirmed
sections.body: There is no information in today’s coverage regarding the injury status of the Japan squad following the recent match against Ireland. No players have been publicly ruled out or confirmed as available for the fixture against France at this time.

sections.heading: France forward line faces changes
sections.body: France winger Damian Penaud will miss the remainder of the Nations Championship matches, including the fixture against Japan, after sustaining a calf injury. France enters the upcoming match following a 42-26 victory over Australia. The French side has demonstrated attacking form, scoring multiple tries in their most recent tournament appearance.

sections.heading: Mid-table positioning for Japan
sections.body: Japan enters this round in 9th position on the tournament log. With a record of one win and one loss, Japan holds a points difference of +1. Securing a result against France is necessary to improve their standing in the table as the competition progresses through the opening month.

sections.heading: Rennie wary of Irish threat as squad prepares for Eden Park
sections.body: Preparations for the upcoming Eden Park showdown are well underway following the side's dominant win over Italy. Head coach Dave Rennie has confirmed the group is focused on the challenge ahead, stating his side is “well aware of Ireland's ability.” With both teams entering the match unbeaten in the tournament, the coaching staff is expected to finalize their tactical approach later this week.

sections.heading: Squad enters training week with focus on recovery
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the recent victory against Italy. The team is currently prioritizing recovery and conditioning sessions to ensure full availability for the weekend. Training intensity is expected to ramp up as the squad prepares for the physical demands of the upcoming Test match, with staff monitoring all players closely throughout the week's sessions.

sections.heading: Momentum builds for Andy Farrell’s side
sections.body: Ireland arrives in Auckland carrying significant momentum, having secured recent victories over Australia and Japan. A notable storyline ahead of the match is the form of Sean Jansen; the 27-year-old has rocketed into contention to face his birth nation after an eye-catching debut in Newcastle. The Irish camp has spoken of the match as a major test for their squad’s growth.

sections.heading: Unbeaten records on the line at Eden Park
sections.body: The All Blacks go into the weekend sitting second on the log, sharing an unbeaten record in the competition with their visitors. With valuable championship points and the team's long winning streak at Eden Park at stake, this fixture represents a critical point in the tournament. Both teams are looking to maintain their trajectory toward the top of the Nations Championship standings.

sections.heading: Rassie rings the changes for Welsh encounter
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has overhauled his side for Saturday’s Test, making 10 changes to the starting XV that beat Scotland. The rotation includes four debutants: wing Jaco Williams, flyhalf Vusi Moyo, lock Ruben van Heerden, and prop Carlu Sadie. Pieter-Steph du Toit retains the captaincy in a side Erasmus describes as an "exciting mix of youth and experience" as he rotates the squad.

sections.heading: Key personnel continue rehabilitation programmes
sections.body: The Springbok camp remains without several senior figures as they focus on conditioning and recovery. Eben Etzebeth, Franco Mostert, Andre Esterhuizen, Canan Moodie, Handré Pollard, and Ox Nché are currently unavailable for selection. These players are continuing their respective rehabilitation programmes, with the medical team managing their return-to-play processes away from the matchday 23 tasked with facing Wales in Durban this weekend.

sections.heading: Wales navigate captaincy blow in Durban
sections.body: Wales enter this fixture following a 35-21 defeat to Argentina, leaving them searching for a reaction against the world champions. Adding to Steve Tandy’s challenges, captain Dewi Lake is set to miss the match through injury. While Wales have shown glimpses of progress, Josh Adams acknowledges that facing the Springboks in South Africa represents the toughest test of their current development under Tandy.

sections.heading: Maintaining momentum at the top
sections.body: South Africa enters the third round of the Nations Championship sitting at the top of the log with two wins from two matches. With a +38 points difference, the Springboks will look to maintain their unbeaten status. A win on Saturday would solidify their position at the summit of the standings before the tournament reaches its midway point, keeping their title ambitions firmly on track.
